perturbation.original_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the Reporting Persons (as such term is defined in the Schedule 13D referred to below) on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13D (including amendments thereto and statements on Schedule 13G) with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such joint filing.
perturbation.changed_text: In accordance with regulations under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the parties of a statement on schedule as deemed necessary with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such filing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific rule '13d-1(k)(1)' and replacing 'Reporting Persons (as such term is defined in the Schedule 13D referred to below)' with 'each of the parties of a statement on schedule as deemed necessary' it creates an omission. This change introduces uncertainty. The original text specifies a particular rule and refers to a precise definition within Schedule 13D, creating certainty about compliance standards. By changing the definition, the rest of the contract is contradicted, which refers to a joint filing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with regulations under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the parties of a statement on schedule as deemed necessary with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such filing. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of any amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the undersigned or contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this agreement as of May 11, 2020. VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory DANIEL KŘETÍNSKÝ /s/ Daniel Kretinsky By: Daniel Kretinsky
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific rule '13d-1(k)(1)' and replaces the defined term 'Reporting Persons (as such term is defined in the Schedule 13D referred to below)' with the vague 'each of the parties of a statement on schedule as deemed necessary'. This creates a contradiction because the original text created certainty and precision about compliance standards by referencing a specific rule and definition. The changed text introduces uncertainty, which directly conflicts with the expectation of clarity for joint filing obligations, as detailed in both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text. The omission of a defined term makes it difficult to determine who the parties are and what schedule they should be filing.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of any amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the undersigned or contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall aim to cooperate on the filing of any amendments, and for the accuracy of the information; however, there is no liability for information concerning the others.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'timely' and 'completeness' creates an omission. The original text definitively assigns responsibility for 'timely filing' and 'completeness and accuracy'. The modified text replaces 'responsible' with 'aim to cooperate' and removes the word 'completeness', weakening the obligation and potentially conflicting with any implicit duty to ensure full regulatory compliance. If elsewhere the document refers to 'complete and timely filings', this alteration introduces a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with regulations under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the parties of a statement on schedule as deemed necessary with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such filing. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall aim to cooperate on the filing of any amendments, and for the accuracy of the information; however, there is no liability for information concerning the others. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this agreement as of May 11, 2020. VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory DANIEL KŘETÍNSKÝ /s/ Daniel Kretinsky By: Daniel Kretinsky
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly assigned responsibility for 'timely filing' and 'completeness and accuracy'. The changed text, which is also present within the contradicted text, weakens this obligation by replacing 'responsible' with 'aim to cooperate' and omitting 'completeness'. This directly contradicts the requirement for adherence to regulations under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, which implies a need for complete and timely filings, as referenced in the Perturbation Explanation. The modified text now creates ambiguity and potential non-compliance, conflicting with the implied regulatory duty within the contradicted text.
